scene: A captain offers you a share in a tin run toward the northern routes Pytheas charted.

choices.label: Take the share
choices.resultText: Months later: tin, profit, and a route in your head worth more than both.

choices.label: Too far, too risky
choices.resultText: The ship sails without you. You check the horizon more than you'd admit.

scene: Ligurian coast pirates demand a 'toll' for safe passage past their headland.

choices.label: Pay the toll
choices.resultText: Cheaper than a hull. Galling all the same.

choices.label: Organize an armed convoy with other traders
choices.resultText: Twelve ships, bristling. The headland stays quiet.

scene: A pouch of your incoming silver rings wrong. Counterfeit obols, well made.

choices.label: Report to the mint magistrates
choices.resultText: You eat the loss; the forger eats a trial.

choices.label: Pass them on in mixed payments
choices.resultText: Someone else's problem now. Several someones.

scene: An Iberian merchant proposes a standing partnership: your wine south, his silver north.

choices.label: Seal it with an oath at the temple
choices.resultText: Witnessed by Artemis and the harbor crowd both.

choices.label: One trial shipment first
choices.resultText: Trust, like cargo, ships in installments.

scene: Your ship limps in after a storm. Half the cargo went over the side to save the hull.

choices.label: Bonus the crew for saving the ship
choices.resultText: Sailors fight for a berth on your ships now.

choices.label: Dock their pay for the lost cargo
choices.resultText: The ledger balances. The crew does not return.

scene: A northern trader offers raw amber - the sun-stone - at a price that means he stole it or needs to vanish.

choices.label: Buy it, ask nothing
choices.resultText: It sells in Syracuse for four times the price. You still wonder.

choices.label: Refuse stolen goods
choices.resultText: Clean hands hold less amber, sleep more.

scene: A rival undercuts you on every amphora in the agora, selling at a loss to break you.

choices.label: Match prices and outlast him
choices.resultText: His backers blink first. The agora is yours again.

choices.label: Start a rumor his oil is rancid
choices.resultText: Effective. Untrue. Effective.

scene: A Carthaginian offers to move your goods through ports closed to Greeks - for a cut and silence.

choices.label: Take the arrangement
choices.resultText: Closed ports open. So does a file with your name, somewhere in Qart-Hadasht.

choices.label: Refuse - some silver costs too much
choices.resultText: He shrugs and finds another Greek. There is always another Greek.

scene: Your overland caravan to the Rhone posts needs guards. Greek mercenaries or Gaulish warriors?

choices.label: Hire Gaulish warriors
choices.resultText: Bandits see torques and long swords, and choose other caravans.

choices.label: Hire Greek mercenaries
choices.resultText: Pricier, but they drill like citizens.

scene: A merchant who owes you a season's profit cannot pay. He offers his daughter's dowry or his ship.

choices.label: Take the ship, forgive the rest
choices.resultText: A fair hull for a fair debt. He works your docks now, loyally.

choices.label: Demand everything owed
choices.resultText: Paid in full. His family's ruin is now part of your reputation.
